subject: general
title: Students don't pay attention during lessons
query_text: Students don't pay attention during lessons

immediate_steps.title: Immediate Action
steps: Break your lesson into 10-minute chunks — alternate between teacher talk and a quick student activity so attention resets naturally
Use a 'call and response' signal (clap a rhythm, students clap it back) to regain focus without raising your voice
Assign rotating 'helper roles' (board writer, question asker, answer checker) so every student knows they may be called on

classroom_activity.title: Classroom Activity — 'Think-Pair-Share'
classroom_activity.activity: After explaining a concept for 5 minutes, pause and pose one clear question (e.g., 'What did we just learn? Tell your neighbour in one sentence.'). Students whisper their answer to the person next to them for 2 minutes, then 3–4 pairs share with the class. This creates low-stakes participation and re-engages wandering minds.
classroom_activity.materials: None — only the lesson content already being taught

inclusion_strategy.title: Inclusion Strategy
inclusion_strategy.strategy: For students who struggle to follow verbal instruction, pair them with a peer buddy who quietly re-explains in the child's home language. This peer support keeps struggling learners engaged without singling them out.

reflection_prompt.title: Reflection
reflection_prompt.question: After trying Think-Pair-Share once, which students surprised you by participating who normally stay silent?

source_citation.title: Source
source_citation.reference: NEP 2020, Section 5.4 — active, participatory pedagogy; NCERT Pedagogical Guidelines — collaborative learning strategies for mixed-ability classrooms (Class 3–5)
